<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504610]] Wikidata name: mui [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mu-i''' (无位・無位, "Mu-i") refers to individuals without a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. This term often specifically denotes those who were employed in {{ill|Executive (government)|lt=government offices|ja|官司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Executive (government)|1=kanshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452091}} and held {{ill|East Asian government position|lt=official positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Asian government position|qq=}} but did not possess a court Rank.
== Overview == Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the correlation between official positions and court Ranks was defined by the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. However, some lower-level positions, particularly those classified as {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}, were excluded from this system. This was particularly common for positions below the {{ill|Four-Tier System of Officials|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-Tier System of Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} and those not treated as {{ill|Hinkan|lt=Hinkan|lt=Pinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pinkan|qq=}}.
For example: - The [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] had a total of 89 positions, but only seven were {{ill|Shikiji Officials|ja|職事官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiji Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職事|1=Shikijikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611227}} with corresponding Ranks. - The {{ill|City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|ja|市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|qq=}} each had 39 positions, but only three—Chief (Sei), Assistant (Yū), and Clerk (Reishi)—were Shikiji Officials. Positions Ranked below Value Chief (Kaichō) were considered {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}} without corresponding Ranks.
These Miscellaneous Officials included not only individuals with low Ranks, such as {{ill|Shōi|ja|初位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}} or {{ill|Hachi-i|ja|八位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachi-i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545368}}, but also individuals entirely without Rank, referred to as "Mu-i."
References in legal texts like the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] confirm the existence of Mu-i officials. For instance: - The {{ill|Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja|衣服令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specified that Mu-i individuals were to wear yellow robes. - The {{ill|Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} required Mu-i individuals to deliver one bundle of firewood as part of their obligations for the annual offerings on the 15th day of the New Year.
Mu-i officials were appointed to Miscellaneous Official positions through the prescribed procedures. They sought opportunities for promotion or acquisition of Rank through diligence and accumulation of merit. While some were fortunate enough to be granted Ranks through the {{ill|Merit Evaluation|ja|考課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merit Evaluation|qq=}} process, this opportunity was limited, and many aspects of their actual circumstances remain unclear.
== References == * {{ill|Takashi Umemura|ja|梅村喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Umemura|1=Takashi Umemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24255034}}, "The Ancient Governmental System and 'Shoku'," in *The Formation Process of the 'Shoku'* (校倉書房, 2011) ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504610]] Wikidata name: mui [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mu-i''' (无位・無位, "Mu-i") refers to individuals without a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. This term often specifically denotes those who were employed in {{ill|Executive (government)|lt=government offices|ja|官司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Executive (government)|1=kanshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452091}} and held {{ill|East Asian government position|lt=official positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Asian government position|qq=}} but did not possess a court Rank.
== Overview == Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the correlation between official positions and court Ranks was defined by the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. However, some lower-level positions, particularly those classified as {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}, were excluded from this system. This was particularly common for positions below the {{ill|Four-Tier System of Officials|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-Tier System of Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} and those not treated as {{ill|Hinkan|lt=Hinkan|lt=Pinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pinkan|qq=}}.
For example: - The [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] had a total of 89 positions, but only seven were {{ill|Shikiji Officials|ja|職事官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiji Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職事|1=Shikijikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611227}} with corresponding Ranks. - The {{ill|City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|ja|市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|qq=}} each had 39 positions, but only three—Chief (Sei), Assistant (Yū), and Clerk (Reishi)—were Shikiji Officials. Positions Ranked below Value Chief (Kaichō) were considered {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}} without corresponding Ranks.
These Miscellaneous Officials included not only individuals with low Ranks, such as {{ill|Shōi|ja|初位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}} or {{ill|Hachi-i|ja|八位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachi-i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545368}}, but also individuals entirely without Rank, referred to as "Mu-i."
References in legal texts like the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] confirm the existence of Mu-i officials. For instance: - The {{ill|Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja|衣服令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specified that Mu-i individuals were to wear yellow robes. - The {{ill|Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} required Mu-i individuals to deliver one bundle of firewood as part of their obligations for the annual offerings on the 15th day of the New Year.
Mu-i officials were appointed to Miscellaneous Official positions through the prescribed procedures. They sought opportunities for promotion or acquisition of Rank through diligence and accumulation of merit. While some were fortunate enough to be granted Ranks through the {{ill|Merit Evaluation|ja|考課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merit Evaluation|qq=}} process, this opportunity was limited, and many aspects of their actual circumstances remain unclear.
== References == * {{ill|Takashi Umemura|ja|梅村喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Umemura|1=Takashi Umemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24255034}}, "The Ancient Governmental System and 'Shoku'," in *The Formation Process of the 'Shoku'* (校倉書房, 2011) ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
{{translated page|ja|无位|version=86401846|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

== References == * {{ill|Takashi Umemura|ja|梅村喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Umemura|1=Takashi Umemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24255034}}, "The Ancient Governmental System and 'Shoku'," in *The Formation Process of the 'Shoku'* (校倉書房, 2011) ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
{{translated page|ja|无位|version=86401846|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{wikidata link|Q11504610}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504610]] Wikidata name: mui [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mu-i''' (无位・無位, "Mu-i") refers to individuals without a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. This term often specifically denotes those who were employed in {{ill|Executive (government)|lt=government offices|ja|官司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Executive (government)|1=kanshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452091}} and held {{ill|East Asian government position|lt=official positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Asian government position|qq=}} but did not possess a court Rank.
== Overview == Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the correlation between official positions and court Ranks was defined by the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. However, some lower-level positions, particularly those classified as {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}, were excluded from this system. This was particularly common for positions below the {{ill|Four-Tier System of Officials|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-Tier System of Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} and those not treated as {{ill|Hinkan|lt=Hinkan|lt=Pinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pinkan|qq=}}.
For example: - The [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] had a total of 89 positions, but only seven were {{ill|Shikiji Officials|ja|職事官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiji Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職事|1=Shikijikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611227}} with corresponding Ranks. - The {{ill|City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|ja|市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|qq=}} each had 39 positions, but only three—Chief (Sei), Assistant (Yū), and Clerk (Reishi)—were Shikiji Officials. Positions Ranked below Value Chief (Kaichō) were considered {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}} without corresponding Ranks.
These Miscellaneous Officials included not only individuals with low Ranks, such as {{ill|Shōi|ja|初位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}} or {{ill|Hachi-i|ja|八位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachi-i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545368}}, but also individuals entirely without Rank, referred to as "Mu-i."
References in legal texts like the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] confirm the existence of Mu-i officials. For instance: - The {{ill|Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja|衣服令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specified that Mu-i individuals were to wear yellow robes. - The {{ill|Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} required Mu-i individuals to deliver one bundle of firewood as part of their obligations for the annual offerings on the 15th day of the New Year.
Mu-i officials were appointed to Miscellaneous Official positions through the prescribed procedures. They sought opportunities for promotion or acquisition of Rank through diligence and accumulation of merit. While some were fortunate enough to be granted Ranks through the {{ill|Merit Evaluation|ja|考課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merit Evaluation|qq=}} process, this opportunity was limited, and many aspects of their actual circumstances remain unclear.
== References == * {{ill|Takashi Umemura|ja|梅村喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Umemura|1=Takashi Umemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24255034}}, "The Ancient Governmental System and 'Shoku'," in *The Formation Process of the 'Shoku'* (校倉書房, 2011) ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
{{translated page|ja|无位|version=86401846|comment=Imported full ja history}}

These Miscellaneous Officials included not only individuals with low Ranks, such as {{ill|Shōi|ja|初位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}} or {{ill|Hachi-i|ja|八位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachi-i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545368}}, but also individuals entirely without Rank, referred to as "Mu-i."
References in legal texts like the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}} confirm the existence of Mu-i officials. For instance: - The {{ill|Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja|衣服令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specified that Mu-i individuals were to wear yellow robes. - The {{ill|Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} required Mu-i individuals to deliver one bundle of firewood as part of their obligations for the annual offerings on the 15th day of the New Year.
Mu-i officials were appointed to Miscellaneous Official positions through the prescribed procedures. They sought opportunities for promotion or acquisition of Rank through diligence and accumulation of merit. While some were fortunate enough to be granted Ranks through the {{ill|Merit Evaluation|ja|考課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merit Evaluation|qq=}} process, this opportunity was limited, and many aspects of their actual circumstances remain unclear.
== References == * {{ill|Takashi Umemura|ja|梅村喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Umemura|1=Takashi Umemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24255034}}, "The Ancient Governmental System and 'Shoku'," in *The Formation Process of the 'Shoku'* (校倉書房, 2011) ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
{{translated page|ja|无位|version=86401846|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:无位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504610]] Wikidata name: mui [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mu-i''' (无位・無位, "Mu-i") refers to individuals without a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}. This term often specifically denotes those who were employed in {{ill|Executive (government)|lt=government offices|ja|官司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Executive (government)|1=kanshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452091}} and held {{ill|East Asian government position|lt=official positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Asian government position|qq=}} but did not possess a court Rank.
== Overview == Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, the correlation between official positions and court Ranks was defined by the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system}}. However, some lower-level positions, particularly those classified as {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}, were excluded from this system. This was particularly common for positions below the {{ill|Four-Tier System of Officials|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-Tier System of Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} and those not treated as {{ill|Hinkan|lt=Hinkan|lt=Pinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pinkan|qq=}}.
For example: - The {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} had a total of 89 positions, but only seven were {{ill|Shikiji Officials|ja|職事官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiji Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職事|1=Shikijikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611227}} with corresponding Ranks. - The {{ill|City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|ja|市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|qq=}} each had 39 positions, but only three—Chief (Sei), Assistant (Yū), and Clerk (Reishi)—were Shikiji Officials. Positions Ranked below Value Chief (Kaichō) were considered {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}} without corresponding Ranks.
These Miscellaneous Officials included not only individuals with low Ranks, such as {{ill|Shōi|ja|初位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}} or {{ill|Hachi-i|ja|八位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachi-i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545368}}, but also individuals entirely without Rank, referred to as "Mu-i."
References in legal texts like the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}} confirm the existence of Mu-i officials. For instance: - The {{ill|Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja|衣服令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specified that Mu-i individuals were to wear yellow robes. - The {{ill|Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} required Mu-i individuals to deliver one bundle of firewood as part of their obligations for the annual offerings on the 15th day of the New Year.
Mu-i officials were appointed to Miscellaneous Official positions through the prescribed procedures. They sought opportunities for promotion or acquisition of Rank through diligence and accumulation of merit. While some were fortunate enough to be granted Ranks through the {{ill|Merit Evaluation|ja|考課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merit Evaluation|qq=}} process, this opportunity was limited, and many aspects of their actual circumstances remain unclear.
== References == * {{ill|Takashi Umemura|ja|梅村喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Umemura|1=Takashi Umemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24255034}}, "The Ancient Governmental System and 'Shoku'," in *The Formation Process of the 'Shoku'* (校倉書房, 2011) ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

'''Mu-i''' (无位・無位, "Mu-i") refers to individuals without a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Court Ranks in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. This term often specifically denotes those who were employed in {{ill|Executive (government)|lt=government offices|ja|官司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Executive (government)|1=kanshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452091}} and held {{ill|East Asian government position|lt=official positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Asian government position|qq=}} but did not possess a court Rank.
== Overview == Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, the correlation between official positions and court Ranks was defined by the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system|1=List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles}}. However, some lower-level positions, particularly those classified as {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}, were excluded from this system. This was particularly common for positions below the {{ill|Four-Tier System of Officials|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-Tier System of Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} and those not treated as {{ill|Hinkan|lt=Hinkan|lt=Pinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pinkan|qq=}}.
For example: - The {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} had a total of 89 positions, but only seven were {{ill|Shikiji Officials|ja|職事官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiji Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職事|1=Shikijikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611227}} with corresponding Ranks. - The {{ill|City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|ja|市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|qq=}} each had 39 positions, but only three—Chief (Sei), Assistant (Yū), and Clerk (Reishi)—were Shikiji Officials. Positions Ranked below Value Chief (Kaichō) were considered {{ill|Miscellaneous Officials|ja|雑任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官人|1=kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}} without corresponding Ranks.
These Miscellaneous Officials included not only individuals with low Ranks, such as {{ill|Shōi|ja|初位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位|1=Senior Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}} or {{ill|Hachi-i|ja|八位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachi-i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545368}}, but also individuals entirely without Rank, referred to as "Mu-i."
References in legal texts like the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}} confirm the existence of Mu-i officials. For instance: - The {{ill|Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja|衣服令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clothing Regulations (Ifuku-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} specified that Mu-i individuals were to wear yellow robes. - The {{ill|Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Regulations (Zatsu-ryō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} required Mu-i individuals to deliver one bundle of firewood as part of their obligations for the annual offerings on the 15th day of the New Year.
Mu-i officials were appointed to Miscellaneous Official positions through the prescribed procedures. They sought opportunities for promotion or acquisition of Rank through diligence and accumulation of merit. While some were fortunate enough to be granted Ranks through the {{ill|Merit Evaluation|ja|考課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merit Evaluation|qq=}} process, this opportunity was limited, and many aspects of their actual circumstances remain unclear.
== References == * {{ill|Takashi Umemura|ja|梅村喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Umemura|1=Takashi Umemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24255034}}, "The Ancient Governmental System and 'Shoku'," in *The Formation Process of the 'Shoku'* (校倉書房, 2011) ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
{{translated page|ja|无位|version=86401846|comment=Imported full ja history}}
